nodes.title: CLIP Text Encode ( Negative Prompt)
widgets_values: text, watermark, logo, signature, oversaturated, artifacts, extra legs, extra feet, extra limbs, duplicate legs, duplicate feet, third leg, third foot, mutated limbs, malformed limbs

### ✅ Recommended Settings for FLUX.2 Klein 9B Distilled

| Parameter          | Value       | Notes |
|--------------------|-------------|-------|
| **CFG Scale**      | `1.0`       | Required for distilled models. Higher values degrade quality. No negative prompt needed.  |
| **Sampler**        | `Euler`     | Best compatibility and quality with this model.  |
| **Scheduler**      | `Simple`    | Works optimally with Euler sampler.  |
| **Steps**          | `4`         | Model is distilled for 4 steps. Can increase to 8 for higher quality.  |
| **Negative Prompt**| Not needed  | Use a “Conditioning Zero Out” dummy node if UI requires it.  |
| **Denoise**        | `1.0`       | For txt2img workflows.  |

---

### 📝 **Key Notes**

- This is a **timestep-distilled** model — it embeds guidance internally, so CFG = 1.0 is mandatory.
- For **photorealistic** results: use `Distilled CFG Scale = 3.5` (if your UI supports it).
- For **artistic/illustrative** styles: try `Distilled CFG Scale = 3.5–6`.
- Avoid “white background” prompts — they can cause blurring.

nodes.title: CLIP Text Encode (Positive Prompt)
widgets_values: remove every piece of clothing the person's wearing, remove shoes completely, preserve pose, preserve proportions, natural anatomy, high detail

nodes.title: List of strings [Qwen Caption + S2 Instruction]
widgets_values: Use this QwenVL outfit description as the authoritative outfit reference. Apply the complete described outfit to the target person in the target pose. Reproduce every visible garment piece and accessory from the description, including upper garments, lower garments, outerwear, lingerie only if described, hosiery, gloves, belts, straps, cutouts, accessories, shoes and boots if described. Match coverage, sleeve length, hem length, pant or skirt length, neckline, collar, material, color, shine, texture, seams, panels and closures. Preserve described skirt and dress hem length and silhouette exactly; do not shorten midi, knee-length, below-knee, calf-length, loose, draped, flared or asymmetrical skirts into mini skirts. Replace S1 underwear and placeholder base clothing only in areas covered by the described outfit. Preserve visible target skin where the outfit description leaves skin visible. Preserve target face, identity, body proportions, hand pose, hand anatomy, camera and background. If gloves are described, render them on the target hands while preserving hand pose and anatomy. Do not copy the reference body or reference pose. Do not invent garment pieces not described.

nodes.title: SAM3 [Target Edit Mask S2 - Qwen Coverage]
widgets_values: neck, shoulders, upper arms, forearms, hands, torso, waist, hips, pelvis, thighs, lower legs, feet

GrowMask [Clean Target Edit Mask S2 - Qwen Coverage]
SAM3 [Outfit Appearance Cutout S2]
widgets_values: outfit, clothing, garment, apparel, dress, gown, bodysuit, jumpsuit, lingerie, bra, panties, top, shirt, blouse, jacket, coat, skirt, pants, trousers, jeans, shorts, leggings, stockings, socks, gloves, boots, shoes, heels, sandals, belt, straps, cutouts, accessories

nodes.title: QwenVL Advanced (GGUF) [Outfit Caption S2 - CPU Low VRAM]
widgets_values:
Describe only the visible outfit and accessories for virtual try-on.

List only what is clearly visible. Do not infer hidden coverage. Do not describe the person, pose, body shape, face, camera or background.

For each visible item, describe:
garment type, body area covered, exposed skin areas, material, color, shine, trim, straps, closures, seams, panels, cutouts, footwear and accessories.

Be precise about coverage:
- bra covers chest only
- briefs cover pelvis only
- stockings cover legs only up to their visible top edge
- gloves cover hands/forearms only if visible
- shoes cover feet only

Be especially precise about skirt or dress hem length and silhouette. If a skirt or dress is visible, state whether it is mini, above-knee, knee-length, below-knee, midi, calf-length, ankle-length, fitted, loose, flared, draped, straight, asymmetrical, or slit. Do not shorten skirts or dresses in the description.

Do not say the outfit is full-body or fully covered unless a continuous full-body garment is visible.
Do not invent hem lengths, knee lengths, sleeves, pants, skirt panels, or closures.

Return one concise technical outfit description. No bullet points.

nodes.title: CLIPTextEncode [Positive S2 - Qwen Outfit Caption]
widgets_values: QwenVL-driven S2 prompt. Fallback only if the List of strings [Qwen Caption + S2 Instruction] link is disconnected.

nodes.title: CLIPTextEncode [Negative S2 - Generic Outfit Errors]
widgets_values: reference pose transfer, copied reference body, copied reference arms, changed arm pose, raised arms, pose change, visible S1 underwear in garment-covered areas, original bra visible where reference garment covers it, original panties visible where reference garment covers them, target photo clothing returns, wrong garment category, different outfit, missing garment pieces, incomplete outfit, forced dress when reference is not a dress, forced pants when reference is not pants, forced sleeves when reference is sleeveless, sleeveless version when reference has sleeves, wrong sleeve length, wrong hem length, wrong pant length, wrong neckline, bare skin where the reference garment should cover, covered skin where the reference outfit is open or transparent, lingerie shape unless the reference outfit is lingerie, separated top and bottom unless the reference outfit is separated, face change, head change, identity change, body shape change, extra limbs, deformed hands, fused hands, skin color change, background change

_vto_notes.RMBG_NODE_CHECK: Node type 'RMBG' comes from comfyui-rmbg (1038lab). If it shows as missing, check node name in ComfyUI Manager and update the type string in the JSON.
_vto_notes.RMBG_MASK_SLOT: RMBG outputs: slot 0 = IMAGE (bg removed), slot 1 = MASK. The workflow uses slot 1.
_vto_notes.LORA_USAGE: All 3 LoRA slots default to weight 0.0 (inactive). Set model_weight + clip_weight to activate. Can use any LoRA from your library.
_vto_notes.DIRECT_PIPE: PreviewImage [S1 Result] feeds VAEEncodeTiled [Base from S1] directly; no save/load intermediate.
_vto_notes.STEP1_MASK_NOTE: RMBG generates full-person mask. Face+hands preserved by low denoise + prompts.
_vto_notes.STEP2_MASK_NOTE: 6.0 keeps a generic target coverage mask for neck, shoulders, arms, hands, torso, hips, legs and feet; QwenVL caption plus S2 prompt decides which regions should actually change.
_vto_notes.step1_denoise_range: 0.4–0.7 → start at 0.60, go lower if face drifts
_vto_notes.step2_denoise_range: 0.5–0.8 → start at 0.65, go higher if outfit too weak
_vto_notes.STEP2_STEPS_NOTE: 5.1 keeps S1 untouched and aligns S2 to the FLUX.2 [klein] 9B 4-step distilled profile.
_vto_notes.debug_order: 1) Test S1 alone + check PreviewImage [S1 Result]  2) In S2 verify PreviewImage [SAM3 Outfit Cutout S2]  3) Then test final transfer  4) Change ONE param at a time
_vto_notes.CURRENT_ARCH: 6.0.2 low-VRAM QwenVL-assisted generic VTON: QwenVL runs CPU/no GPU layers, VRAM is cleared after captioning, then SAM3 and Flux continue normally.
_vto_notes.OUTFIT_REFERENCE_NOTE: 6.0.1 uses QwenVL Advanced GGUF to caption the outfit reference. The caption should list all garment pieces, coverage, lengths, material, color, shine, straps, cutouts, accessories and footwear, and should be especially precise about skirt/dress hem length and silhouette.
_vto_notes.POSE_STABILITY_NOTE: If identity or pose drifts, lower S2 denoise toward 0.65 or FluxGuidance toward 2.0. Do not reintroduce spatial latent compositing from the outfit reference.
_vto_notes.MASK_CLEANUP_NOTE: VTONMaskCleanup thresholds, closes gaps, fills holes, and removes small SAM3 islands. In 5.9 keep_largest remains disabled so disconnected garment coverage components can survive cleanup.
_vto_notes.S2_DENOISE_NOTE: 6.0 uses S2 denoise 0.72 with FluxGuidance 2.6 because the QwenVL caption should provide stronger outfit-specific guidance without hardcoding a garment category.
_vto_notes.SKIRT_LENGTH_NOTE: 6.0.1 specifically tells QwenVL and S2 not to shorten described knee-length, below-knee, midi, calf-length, loose, draped, flared or asymmetrical skirts into mini skirts.
_vto_notes.LOW_VRAM_NOTE: 6.0.2 changes QwenVL to CPU, ctx 4096, n_batch 128, gpu_layers 0 and image_max_tokens 2048, and inserts VRAM Debug [After Qwen Caption] before S2 text conditioning to reduce repeated-run OOM on 8GB GPUs.
